subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Raw and processed data are communicated with clear headings, units and consistent significant figures.
Graphs include trendlines, equations and uncertainty bars, enhancing precision.
Uncertainties are propagated for a representative trial and summarized effectively.
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation is not shown for every trial and combined uncertainty on the gradient is omitted.
Numerical inconsistencies (unit conversion for slope, negative power sign) introduce inaccuracies.
Table layouts are overly dense, hindering rapid interpretation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data processing is generally clear and precise, with appropriate formulas and presentations. However, omissions in full uncertainty propagation, unit inconsistencies, and some calculation errors prevent a top-band, yielding a score of 5.

strengths: Research question is framed within a precise industrial context and justified with literature.
Procedures are detailed with diagrams, variable tables and full material lists enabling reproducibility.
Key methodological considerations (heat losses, coil resistance drift, water purity, controls) are anticipated and mitigated.
weaknesses: Minor digression on historical background dilutes focus.
Small ambiguities remain regarding coil resistance re-measurement and instrument tolerances.
Uncertainty ranges for timing and volume measurements are not explicitly stated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The design situates the research question in a specific, real-world context and supplies nearly all details needed for replication. Methodological considerations are thoroughly explained. Only minor omissions (e.g. calibration tolerances, redundant historical detail) prevent lowering from a perfect score.

strengths: Conclusion is directly justified by analytical results and strong RÂ² value.
Comparison to literature and peer investigations is relevant and wellâcontextualized.
Applied findings to real-world desalination and cooling contexts, demonstrating scientific maturity.
weaknesses: Discussion of systematic errors (e.g. coil drift) lacks quantified impact.
A few efficiency statements are inferential rather than directly evidenced.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is fully consistent with analysis, justified by quantitative metrics and comparison to accepted scientific context. Minor gaps in error-analysis depth do not outweigh the thorough support and relevance, meriting the maximum score.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (water source change, coil resistance variation).
Proposed improvements are realistic, relevant and linked to accuracy and consistency gains.
Reflective insight shows understanding of how changes would affect data quality.
weaknesses: Impact of each weakness is discussed qualitatively rather than quantified.
Suggestions lack prioritization or feasibility analysis for implementation.
Relative magnitude of the improvementsâ benefits is not clearly evaluated.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes pertinent limitations and presents feasible improvements. While the discussion explains why changes matter, the absence of quantitative impact estimates and prioritization keeps this from a perfect band, justifying a score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is clearly phrased, focused and embedded in a specific industrial context (use of saline water for cooling/mining).  The introduction explains why specific-heat capacity matters, cites literature and links directly to the chosen salt concentrations, giving the reader an immediate, well-defined background.  This corresponds to the descriptor of a âspecific and appropriate contextâ, though a few historical digressions are not strictly necessary, so a mid-high score of 5 is most fitting.

Uncertainties are propagated in detail for one representative trial and quoted in the summary table, showing solid understanding.  However, only a sample calculation is given; propagation for other trials appears assumed, and instrument resolution justification is patchy.  Significant omissions (e.g. no combined uncertainty on gradient) place it at the lower end of band 3-4 â 3.

The student recognises key considerations (heat losses, emissivity, coil resistance drift, water purity, controlled variables) and justifies three repeats across seven concentrations.  Explanations of why each control matters show understanding, yet some aspects (coil resistance variation, manual-stirrer heat capacity) are acknowledged only after data collection and not in the initial design.  Therefore methodological considerations are described rather than fully explained â upper band 3-4.

Two clear weaknesses are identified (switch from distilled to tap water; changing coil resistance) and their effects on data spread are discussed qualitatively.  The explanation of impact is partialâno quantitative estimate of error magnitudeâso the strand meets the descriptor for âdescribes specific weaknessesâ rather than fully explaining relative impact.  Score 3.

Step-by-step procedures, apparatus diagrams, variable table and full material list allow another student to rerun the investigation with minimal doubt.  Voltmeter/ammeter ranges, mass and volume readings, timing and stirring intervals are all stated.  Minor ambiguities remain (e.g. resistance not re-measured each trial, unclear timing of radiation temperature readings), so the strand falls short of the perfect 6, but fulfils the requirement for full reproducibility â 5.

Energy inputs, heat losses and specific heat are calculated using appropriate formulas, and averages/graphed relationships are produced.  Yet some numerical inconsistencies exist (e.g. negative power sign, very large percentage slope uncertainty) and the linear fit uses 100 ml rather than kg units without conversion.  Processing is therefore adequate but contains significant inaccuracies â score 3.

Realistic improvements are proposed (use only distilled water, measure coil resistance each trial, replace coil).  Explanations focus on how these changes would improve accuracy/consistency, but remain brief and lack prioritisation or feasibility analysis.  This aligns with ârelevant improvements are describedâ (band 3-4), lower half â 3.

Raw-data tables include headings, units and uncertainties; processed-data tables add calculated values and error propagation.  Graphs are labelled and include trend equations.  Layout is occasionally cluttered, but overall the communication is clear OR precise; most values carry consistent sig. figs.  Hence band 3-4 with a score of 4.

The conclusion is compared to published data and a peer IA, noting agreement in trend and closeness of specific-heat values; the student also links findings to desalination/cooling contexts.  These comparisons are relevant and partly justified, though they rely on a single secondary source and do not discuss theoretical models in depth.  Fits well in band 3-4 with a score of 4.

The student states an inverse linear relationship and quantifies it with RÂ², directly linking to processed data.  The conclusion aligns with the numerical trend and comments on proximity of 4419 J kgâ»Â¹ Â°Câ»Â¹ to the literature 4186.  Minor logical leaps (e.g. efficiency statements) stop short of a fully justified, exhaustive discussion, so the middle of band 3-4 (score 4) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The lengthy historical digression on Joseph Black, while interesting, dilutes the focus; consider condensing to essential theory to maintain clarity and relevance.
Cite more recent primary literature when defining specific-heat capacity rather than relying primarily on online encyclopaedias to strengthen the academic rigour.
The definition of the resultant energy (Qâresultantâ) omits precise description of how Qâradiationâ is measured; clarify data-collection steps to improve reproducibility.

The research question is precisely framed within an industrial context (cooling/mining), linking specific-heat capacity relevance to saline applications, which strengthens the studyâs focus and real-world justification.
Raw-data tables include uncertainties and units for all measured quantities, demonstrating clear communication of data recording and processing.
The heating-coil resistance is assumed constant at 1.36 Î© for all trials despite breakdowns; this introduces systematic error and should be addressed.
Uncertainty calculations are demonstrated only for trial 5; extend propagation to all trials and include combined uncertainty on the gradient for completeness.
When deriving Qâsuppliedâ, include the alternative expression P=VI as a cross-check to validate the power calculation, enhancing methodological rigour.
The controlled-variables table omits uncertainty ranges for timing and volume measurements; add instrument precisions (Â± values) to fully account for random errors.
Consider detailing calibration ranges and tolerances for each apparatus component (e.g., voltmeter, ammeter) to ensure measurements are traceable and reliable.
The experimental-procedure steps are impressively detailed, providing clear reproducibility; this thoroughness strengthens the research design.
Slope units are inconsistent: concentration in g should be converted to kg in the calculation of specific-heat units; adjust units or annotate the conversion.
Explain how the y-intercept (4 419 J kgâ»Â¹ Â°Câ»Â¹) physically represents the specificâheat capacity of pure water, to deepen the conclusionâs linkage to theory.
The evaluation table effectively identifies specific strengths in methodology, demonstrating reflective insight into experimental design.
Clarify the assumption that emissivity and the Stefan-Boltzmann constant have negligible uncertainties, and discuss their potential impact.
The processed-data table is exhaustive and integrates uncertainties and averages, facilitating interpretation of trends.
The table layout is excessively dense, hindering clarity; consider splitting into separate tables or highlighting key columns to improve readability.
The plotted trend line with RÂ²=0.948 effectively communicates the strong linear relationship, supporting the data analysis.
The concluding discussion on systematic errors is vague; specify magnitudes (e.g., coil drift error) to strengthen the evaluation of impact on results.
Suggested improvements (e.g., using only distilled water) are realistic, but lack explanation of feasibility or prioritization; expand to justify their implementation.
The conclusion is well supported by the analysis and hypothesis, clearly summarizing the inverse relationship between salt concentration and specific-heat capacity.
The comparison with Lucy Quâs findings is insightful, contextualizing results against peer research and IB-appropriate scientific standards.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
